definition: Payment-in-kind toggle activation has increased from 5-8% to 12-18% of leveraged loans, representing the earliest observable symptom of monetary transmission reaching leveraged borrowers. PIK capitalization is economically equivalent to issuing new debt to service existing debt. Public default rates (~2-3%) significantly understate true credit stress.
evidence: PIK economically equivalent to new debt issuance for debt service
Public default rates 2-3% understate true stress
Identified as single most actionable data point in debates
open_questions: What is the total PIK balance across the leveraged loan market?
At what PIK activation rate does the system tip into default cascades?
How do exact percentages vary by data source?
